data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260514T090626-d4274524/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260514T090626-d4274524/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260514T090626-d4274524/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260514T090626-d4274524/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260514T090626-d4274524/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-96ccd9c0">
    <title>test: measure coverage and enforce a ratchet</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
The default DDx test path currently answers only pass/fail. It does not produce an aggregate coverage number, does not persist a baseline, and does not fail when coverage regresses. That made it easy to miss that the interrupted work/watch tracker-noise path had no real integration coverage until production behavior exposed it.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- Makefile:23-24 delegates `make test` to `cli-test` without any coverage gate.
- Makefile:59-61 delegates CLI tests to `cd cli && $(MAKE) test`, so root-level default testing inherits the CLI target behavior.
- cli/Makefile:111-114 runs `go test -v ./...` with no `-coverprofile`, aggregate coverage parsing, baseline comparison, or ratchet persistence.
- cli/Makefile:116-120 has `test-full` run `go test -race -cover ./...`, but it only prints package coverage and has no enforced ratchet.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a coverage measurement and ratchet workflow for the default Go test suite. The implementation must read its baseline/threshold from a checked-in repo config or data file, not from hardcoded defaults in Go source. Add a Make target that runs coverage, reports aggregate coverage, compares it to the configured baseline, and fails on regression. Decide whether `make test` should invoke the ratchet directly or whether CI/default-test documentation should make the ratchet target mandatory; if not wired into `make test`, document the reason in the bead implementation.

NON-SCOPE
Do not require live harness tests, perf tests, or networked integration tests for the coverage ratchet. Do not make developers update a baseline implicitly during ordinary test runs. Do not hide coverage failures behind verbose-only output. Do not manually edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- Makefile
- cli/Makefile
- cli/tools/ or cli/internal/ test-support package for coverage-ratchet parsing/comparison
- a checked-in coverage ratchet config/baseline file under cli/ or .ddx/

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- Fizeau routing policy
- worker execution semantics
- live_harness and perf test inclusion policy
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Add `TestCoverageRatchetReadsBaselineFromConfig` proving the ratchet threshold/baseline is loaded from a checked-in config/data file and is not a compiled fallback.
2. Add `TestCoverageRatchetFailsBelowBaseline` proving a measured aggregate below the configured baseline returns a non-zero/failing result with a useful message.
3. Add `TestCoverageRatchetAllowsEqualOrImprovedCoverage` proving equal or improved measured coverage passes without updating the baseline.
4. Add a reproducible command, for example `cd cli && make coverage-ratchet`, that runs `go test` with a cover profile, computes aggregate coverage, and enforces the configured ratchet.
5. Run `cd cli && go test ./...` and the new coverage-ratchet command.
6. Run `lefthook run pre-commit`.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:test, area:ci, kind:task, quality, coverage, ratchet</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="4b88cbe275472a7103247d540a7d62ef65fdde5e" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260514T090626-d4274524"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

Lefthook green; ratchet workflow verified end to end. The pre-existing `TestServerWorker_WiresPreClaimIntakeHook` failure on base rev `4b88cbe27` is unrelated (reproduced with my changes stashed).
